title: Cross-Session Persistence
constraints: The persistence format must be human-readable (e.g., JSON)
Loading previous session context must happen automatically on startup
The agent must gracefully handle a missing or empty persistence file
hints: Serialize the memory store to a JSON file after each turn and load it on startup
Use a session ID to separate different users' memory files
On startup, check if a persistence file exists; if so, load it and inject a summary of the previous session into context

starterCode.langchain:
from langchain_openai import ChatOpenAI
from langchain_core.messages import HumanMessage, AIMessage, SystemMessage

llm = ChatOpenAI(model="gpt-4o-mini")

# BUG: Memory is purely in-memory — lost when the process restarts
history = [SystemMessage(content="You are a helpful assistant.")]

def chat(user_input: str) -> str:
    history.append(HumanMessage(content=user_input))
    response = llm.invoke(history)
    history.append(response)
    return response.content

# Session 1
print("=== Session 1 ===")
print(chat("My name is Jordan."))
print(chat("I'm working on Project Phoenix."))
print(chat("The deadline is March 15th."))

# Simulate process restart — all memory is lost
history = [SystemMessage(content="You are a helpful assistant.")]

# Session 2
print("\n=== Session 2 (after restart) ===")
print(chat("What's my name?"))
print(chat("What project am I working on?"))
starterCode.crewai:
from crewai import Agent, Task, Crew
from crewai import LLM

llm = LLM(model="gpt-4o-mini")

agent = Agent(
    role="Assistant",
    goal="Help users with their questions",
    backstory="You are a helpful assistant.",
    llm=llm,
)

# BUG: Memory is purely in-memory — lost when the process restarts
history = []

def chat(user_input: str) -> str:
    history.append(f"User: {user_input}")
    context = "\n".join(history)
    task = Task(
        description=f"Continue this conversation:\n{context}\n\nRespond to the latest message.",
        expected_output="A helpful response",
        agent=agent,
    )
    crew = Crew(agents=[agent], tasks=[task])
    result = str(crew.kickoff())
    history.append(f"Assistant: {result}")
    return result

# Session 1
print("=== Session 1 ===")
print(chat("My name is Jordan."))
print(chat("I'm working on Project Phoenix."))
print(chat("The deadline is March 15th."))

# Simulate process restart — all memory is lost
history = []

# Session 2
print("\n=== Session 2 (after restart) ===")
print(chat("What's my name?"))
print(chat("What project am I working on?"))
starterCode.openai-agents-sdk:
from agents import Agent, Runner

agent = Agent(
    name="Assistant",
    instructions="You are a helpful assistant.",
)

# BUG: Memory is purely in-memory — lost when the process restarts
history = []

def chat(user_input: str) -> str:
    history.append({"role": "user", "content": user_input})
    result = Runner.run_sync(agent, history)
    response_text = result.final_output
    history.append({"role": "assistant", "content": response_text})
    return response_text

# Session 1
print("=== Session 1 ===")
print(chat("My name is Jordan."))
print(chat("I'm working on Project Phoenix."))
print(chat("The deadline is March 15th."))

# Simulate process restart — all memory is lost
history = []

# Session 2
print("\n=== Session 2 (after restart) ===")
print(chat("What's my name?"))
print(chat("What project am I working on?"))

evaluationCriteria.label: Memory survives restart
evaluationCriteria.description: The agent can recall information from a previous session after a process restart

evaluationCriteria.label: Loads automatically on startup
evaluationCriteria.description: Previous session context is loaded automatically without manual intervention

evaluationCriteria.label: Handles cold start gracefully
evaluationCriteria.description: When no previous session exists, the agent starts fresh without errors
